data.title: SoFi: I'm Not Expecting Major Gains Anytime Soon (Technical Analysis)
data.description:
Intro

Since my holding rating back at the start of February, SoFi Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:SOFI) has seen declines of around 10%. I believe the stock continues to be a hold. In the below analysis, most technicals indicate uncertainty and indecision in the stock, and so the outlook conveyed is around neutral. For the fundamentals, Q1 earnings were strong and guidance for the rest of the year is robust. However, the stock seems to be fairly valued, as the P/B ratio is sitting at an elevated position relative to historic levels and to peers in the Financials sector. As a result of technical uncertainty and the fundamental setup being fair, I believe a reiteration of the hold rating is most appropriate.

Technicals

Charting

SoFi was in a downtrend beginning in late January to until late April, when it broke out on extraordinary volume. The stock briefly slid back below, but by early to mid-May, it was clear the downtrend was over. A nearer term uptrend began to form back at the early April lows and the trend line supported the stock until mid-May when it was broken. Since then, there has been horizontal drift and so SoFi doesn't seem to be in much of a trend currently.

There are three support levels identified in addition to the former downtrend line. The nearest is at around 12.5 as that area was support briefly in early March and in early May while being resistance in early April. The next support level is at 11.3 as a late April gap up occurred there. This level was previously support in March as well. Lastly, there is distant support all the way back at the bullish engulfing pattern in early April.

For resistance, there are also three relevant levels in addition to the former uptrend line, with the first at around 13.9. This area was resistance on the previous trading day and in late March, while being support back in January and February. Moving up, 14.7 might be another significant area as support was found there in December and early February while resistance was experienced there in May. Lastly, 16.7 could be resistance, as this level was an obstacle back in late December and mid-February.

Takeaway: In my view, with support and resistance rather balanced and the stock not in an obvious trend, the chart is currently neutral.

Moving Averages

It is clear that long term bullish momentum has been receding for quite a while, with the gap between the 50 day and 200 day SMAs shrinking since early February. If you look closely, a death cross has just occurred. However, with the stock now trading a notable amount above both SMAs, this may be a false signal. The 50-day SMA may just be bouncing off the 200-day SMA as a sign of resilient bulls.

For the near term, the 9 day and 21 day EMAs had a bullish crossover back in late April. The deviation between these lines generally increased until mid-May. After a pullback in the 9 day EMA to reflect the decreased upward momentum, the deviation between the EMAs is now holding quite steady and so near term strength remains in SoFi stock.

The stock broke above the Bollinger Bands midline in late April. This showed the downtrend was weakening and was at risk of ending. It also showed that a new uptrend was potentially beginning. In late May, the stock broke back below the midline, confirming the end of the uptrend. However, since then, the stock has been trading around the midline as an indication of uncertainty rather than bearishness.

Takeaway: I believe the moving averages show uncertainty, with the SMA death cross being questionable and the Bollinger Bands midline not acting as support nor resistance.

Indicators

At around mid-April, the MACD had a bullish crossover. Momentum started to recede by the end of that month, however, and a bearish crossover occurred in late May. Since then, the indicator line and signal line have been nearly inseparable, as you can see by the very flat histogram. This is another signal of uncertainty and indecision in the stock. The MACD did confirm the early April bullish engulfing pattern, with slight positive divergence relative to its March trough.

The RSI broke its uptrend that started at early April lows in mid to late May, showing that the period of strength had ended. Still, the indicator has largely held above 50 since then, and the current reading of 51.74 shows that the bulls remain in control by a thin margin. Similar to the MACD, the RSI seems to have held a hair above its March trough during its early April low, and so the bullish reversal was confirmed.

The stochastics displayed a relatively early bearish signal in early to mid-May to show that the bull run was over. The %K crossed below the %D and both lines went on to break below 50. While this showed net bearish control, the stochastics didn't drop below 20 in late May, and so the bears were never really dominant. A bullish crossover occurred at the very end of May, and so the bulls have started to recover as of late.

Takeaway: I would say the indicators also show indecision in the near term, as there are mixed signals. For the long term, the April low is likely to hold due to MACD and RSI confirmation.

Fibonacci Retracement

Like the other technicals discussed so far, the Fibonacci Retracement shows the stock is indecisive on its next major move. The rebound from early April saw some resistance at the 23.6% retracement, but the 38.2% retracement breakout was rather straightforward. In fact, the stock surged above 50% retracement as well. After finally finding resistance at the 61.8% retracement, the stock receded below the 50% retracement. Since then, SoFi has been flirting with the 50% retracement, but no real breakouts or significant pullbacks from that level have occurred. Therefore, currently the odds of a continued retracement are nearly the same as the odds of a resumption of the down move in my view.

Fundamentals

Earnings

SoFi reported their 2025 Q1 earnings back on April 29th and showed strong results overall. Adjusted net revenue was reported to be $770.720 million, up 33% from the prior year period. Their Financial Services segment saw the most impressive growth at around 100% as revenues surged to $303 million. They attribute this to factors such as strong adoption of SoFi Money and the expansion of their Loan Platform Business. For adjusted EBITDA, they reported $210.337 million, up 46% YoY. This figure along with strong contribution profit in each of their three segments show that the business as a whole is performing well. As for adjusted EPS, they reported $0.06, up from the year ago quarter's $0.02. This also beat consensus estimates by $0.03 showing relative outperformance. As for their operations, SoFi saw a record 800,000 new members join in their Q1, representing YoY growth of 34% to 10.9 million. As you can see above, this is in-line with 2024's full-year members growth rate. They also had 1.2 million new products, up 35% YoY to 15.9 million products. Also shown above, this growth rate is an acceleration versus 2024 full year, and so their business seems to be booming at this point.

Guidance

For their Q2, SoFi is expecting to see adjusted net revenues of between $785 million and $805 million. This would represent midpoint YoY growth of 33.2%, basically unchanged from their Q1 growth rate. For adjusted EBITDA, they project a range of $200 million to $210 million. At the midpoint, this guidance would imply growth of 48.7%, an acceleration from Q1's 46%. Therefore, I would consider Q2 guidance to be strong. For the full year, SoFi raised their guidance to the figures shown above. Adjusted revenue growth is implied to be 25.6% at the midpoint, unchanged from 2024's 26% full-year growth rate. For adjusted EBITDA, they are expecting a margin of 27% representing a slight improvement from 2024. Lastly, SoFi projects that they will add a minimum of 2.8 million new members in 2025 for a growth rate of 28%. This is a slowdown from 2024, but nonetheless shows the outlook is robust.

Valuation

The P/B ratio achieved a three-year high early this year. After a period of contraction and then a rebound, the multiple finds itself at 2.2. I believe this valuation is justified. As I discussed earlier, Q1 earnings for SoFi were strong as a whole. Financial metrics saw impressive growth, and operational metrics like member count also saw strength. Looking ahead, Q2 guidance showed stable revenue growth and accelerating EBITDA growth relative to their Q1. Full year financial guidance was also strong, and 28% projected member growth is still respectable. Therefore, as a whole, the outlook for SoFi's business is bright even as geopolitical and macroeconomic headwinds flare. I believe this impressive performance amidst headwinds justifies this relatively elevated P/B ratio. The multiple is at a significantly higher reading than the Financials sector median P/B of 1.2, but given their innovative business model and robust growth, I believe this valuation gap is not unjustified. I conclude that SoFi is around fairly valued at current levels.

Conclusion

Overall, I am expecting SoFi to trade relatively horizontally from here. The technicals show an uncertain outlook, as there are many mixed signals throughout my analysis. For the fundamentals, strong Q1 earnings and raised guidance for the full year 2025 indicates that impressive business performance is expected to continue. Q2 guidance shows that adjusted EBITDA growth will accelerate, and full year adjusted EBITDA margin is expected to improve from 2024. In my view, these strong fundamentals are able to justify the elevated P/B ratio, showing that the stock is fairly valued. As a result of technical uncertainty and a neutral fundamental setup, I believe SoFi is still only a hold.
